extraction.article_title: Adobe Reader Zero-Day Exploited via Malicious PDFs Since December 2025

ttps.description: User Execution: attackers rely on social engineering via malicious PDF invoices and topical lures to get victims to open a weaponized document.
Input Capture: malicious PDF JavaScript and Acrobat API abuse can be used to collect sensitive local information from the host or reader environment.
Ingress Tool Transfer: the PDF can contact an external server to retrieve additional JavaScript or payload stages.
Obfuscated Files or Information: the embedded JavaScript is described as obfuscated to hinder analysis.
Exploitation for Client Execution: a zero-day in Adobe Reader is used to trigger privileged Acrobat APIs and potentially follow-on code execution.

attack_chain: Deliver malicious PDF with invoice-style or current-events lure.
Victim opens the PDF in Adobe Reader.
Embedded obfuscated JavaScript executes and abuses privileged Acrobat APIs.
Payload harvests local/system information and fingerprints the environment.
Payload exfiltrates collected data to a remote server.
Server may return additional JavaScript or next-stage exploit content.
Potential follow-on stage aims for RCE and sandbox escape.

proposed_vectors.name_en: Malicious PDF document lure with embedded Acrobat JavaScript
proposed_vectors.name_ko: 악성 PDF 문서 유인과 내장 Acrobat JavaScript
